title: Citrus room spray
subtitle: A lemon and sweet orange room spray in a vodka-water base
excerpt: A bright citrus room spray made from lemon and sweet orange essential oils in a vodka-and-water base. The vodka acts as a solubiliser to disperse the essential oils evenly in the spray. A few spritzes freshen a room, fabric, or upholstery without the synthetic notes of commercial air fresheners.
sourceNotes: Essential Oil Safety, Tisserand and Young (2014), citrus EO inhalation limits. Vodka as a natural EO solubiliser and preservative in water-based sprays, general perfumery practice.

content.text: A citrus room spray. Essential oils and water do not mix without an emulsifier or a solubiliser. Vodka at 40% ABV (ethanol) dissolves essential oils completely; the dissolved oils then disperse throughout the water when shaken. At 10% of the formula, the vodka is enough to solubilise this quantity of essential oil and keep the blend stable between shakes. Higher-proof spirit (75%+ ABV perfumer's alcohol) gives a cleaner result and better EO suspension, but 40% vodka works well for a home room spray used within 12 months.

Lemon essential oil is cold-pressed from the peel and phototoxic: do not spray onto skin and then go into direct sunlight. For a room and fabric spray this is not a concern; the spray is not skin-applied. Sweet orange essential oil rounds the lemon's sharp top note into a fuller citrus profile. Lemon EO has a shorter shelf life than most essential oils; use the spray within 12 months from the date the bottle was filled.

items.name: Vodka
items.prepNote: 40% ABV vodka (standard supermarket vodka). 10% of the spray. Acts as the solubiliser and a mild preservative. The brand is irrelevant; any cheap 40% vodka works.

items.name: Distilled water
items.prepNote: distilled or deionised water. 88% of the spray. Tap water leaves mineral deposits on fabrics over time; distilled water avoids this.

items.name: Lemon essential oil
items.prepNote: Citrus limon lemon essential oil (cold-pressed). 1.2% of the spray. The primary citrus note; sharp, bright, zesty. Approximately 30 drops.

items.name: Sweet orange essential oil
items.prepNote: Citrus sinensis sweet orange essential oil. 0.8% of the spray. Softens and rounds the lemon top note.

content.text: Measure the vodka into the spray bottle first. Add both essential oils directly to the vodka and swirl to dissolve; the alcohol solubilises the oils before the water is added. Add the distilled water. Fit the trigger-spray top and shake well for 15 seconds. Label with the contents and batch date, and note to shake before each use.

Shake the bottle. Spritz 2-4 times into the centre of a room, or hold 30 cm from fabric and spray once onto curtains, cushions, or upholstery. Do not spray directly onto silk, satin, or delicate fabrics; test on an inside seam first. The citrus scent is bright immediately and settles to a softer background note within 20-30 minutes.

12 months in an amber spray bottle. Citrus essential oils are among the most photosensitive; keep the bottle in a cupboard or drawer between uses. If the spray loses its bright top note (smelling flat or slightly oxidised), the lemon oil has degraded; make a fresh batch.
